1: With my mouth will I make known thy faithfulness to all generations.
2: Thy faithfulness shalt thou establish in the very heavens.
3: I have sworn unto David my servant,
4: And build up thy throne to all generations. Selah. Selah.
5: Thy faithfulness also in the congregation of the saints.
6: Can be likened unto the LORD LORD ?
7: And to be had in reverence of all them that are them that are about him.
8: Or to thy faithfulness round about thee?
9: When the waves thereof arise, thou stillest them.
10: Thou hast scattered thine enemies with thy strong arm.
11: As for As for the world and the fulness thereof, thou hast founded them.
12: Tabor and Hermon shall rejoice in thy name.
13: Strong is thy hand, and and high is thy right hand.
14: Mercy and truth shall go before thy face.
15: They shall walk, O LORD LORD , in the light of thy countenance.
16: And in thy righteousness shall they be exalted.
17: And in thy favour our horn shall be exalted.
18: And the Holy One of Israel is is our King.
19: I have laid help upon one that is one that is mighty; I have exalted one one chosen out of the people.
20: With my holy oil have I anointed him:
21: Mine arm also shall strengthen him.
22: Nor the son of wickedness afflict him.
23: And plague them that hate him.
24: And in my name shall his horn be exalted.
25: And his right hand in the rivers.
26: My God, and the rock of my salvation.
27: Higher than the kings of the earth.
28: And my covenant shall stand fast with him.
29: And his throne as the days of heaven.
30: And walk not in my judgments;
31: If they break my statutes, and keep not my commandments;
32: Then will I visit their transgression with the rod, and their iniquity with stripes.
33: Nor suffer my faithfulness to fail.
34: My covenant will I not break, nor alter the thing that is gone out of my lips.
35: Once have I sworn by my holiness that I will not lie unto David.
36: And his throne as the sun before me.
37: And as as a faithful witness in heaven. Selah. Selah.
38: Thou hast been wroth with thine anointed.
39: Thou hast profaned his crown by casting it by casting it to the ground.
40: Thou hast brought his strong holds to ruin.
41: He is a reproach to his neighbours.
42: Thou hast made all his enemies to rejoice.
43: And hast not made him to stand in the battle.
44: And cast his throne down to the ground.
45: Thou hast covered him with shame. Selah. Selah.
46: Shall thy wrath burn like fire?
47: Wherefore hast thou made all men in vain?
48: Shall he deliver his soul from the hand of the grave? Selah. Selah.
49: Which Which thou swarest unto David in thy truth?
50: How How I do bear in my bosom the reproach of the reproach of all the mighty people;
51: Wherewith they have reproached the footsteps of thine anointed.
